commit_scope: Stamp the deployed SHA into the config file consumed by the public web health proxy during Hostinger deploys.

commands: tmpdir=$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p "$tmpdir/api/config"; cp api/config/api.json "$tmpdir/api/config/api.json"; TARGET_SHA=0123456789abcdef REPO_DIR="$tmpdir" python3 - <<'PY' ... PY; jq -e --arg sha 0123456789abcdef '.deployed_sha == $sha and .web.deployed_sha == $sha and .web.updated_at == $sha' "$tmpdir/api/config/api.json" >/dev/null; rm -rf "$tmpdir"
local_validation.summary: The deploy script parses correctly and the SHA-stamping logic updates root deployed_sha plus web.deployed_sha and web.updated_at in a temporary config copy.

deploy_validation.details: Pending Hostinger auto-deploy and strict public web health-proxy SHA validation after merge.

phase_gate.reason: Local deploy-script proof is complete; CI and public deploy proof remain pending.

evidence_refs: Strict public verification passed API SHA parity at 91e9a592a29da430d21011d14c1272992f0fdd79 but failed because the web health proxy exposed deployed_sha=unknown.
The web health proxy reads WEB_CONFIG.deployedSha from api/config/api.json, while the Hostinger deploy script only stamped .env before this change.
The deploy script now stamps the target SHA into the config consumed by the web route before docker compose build/up.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: After the next Hostinger deploy, /api/health-proxy reports web.deployed_sha equal to /api/gates/main-head deployed_sha.
test_flows: Strict public deploy verification with VERIFY_REQUIRE_WEB_HEALTH_PROXY_SHA=1
